title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Christine Redfern describes the deceased Elena Marshall as an unintelligent, useless parasite involved in "dirty affairs" such as extortion and jealousy. When Colonel Weston questions her use of the word "extortion," Christine admits she accidentally overheard a man with a muffled, unrecognizable voice extorting Mrs. Marshall three days prior while she was playing bridge.

Following Christine's departure, Inspector Cargraves, Colonel Weston, Hercule Poirot, and Detective Inspector Colgate discuss the possibility that Mrs. Marshall was meeting a blackmailer at Fairy Bay. They note the location is ideal for a secret meeting because it is deserted, contains a hidden cave known as the Fairy Cave, and is accessible only by a vertical steel ladder. Weston narrows the potential suspects staying at the hotel to Mr. Gardner, Major Barry, Mr. Horace Blatt, and Reverend Stephen Lane, noting that the latter three were out during the morning.

Colonel Weston then summons the American couple, the Gardners, for questioning. Mrs. Gardner claims that she and her husband were on the beach all morning. She expresses disdain for Mrs. Marshall, calling her dim-witted and uncivilized, and suggests that Mr. Marshall should have married the intelligent and successful Rosamond Darnely instead. Mrs. Gardner remarks that it was obvious to everyone how much Darnely loved Mr. Marshall.
